User.title: User
User.description: Represents a student user account on the PrepStack platform. Stores profile information and references to personalized data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

userId.description: A unique 10-character alphanumeric user ID chosen by the user for login purposes. (Must be unique across all users).

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

lastLogin.description: Timestamp of the user's last successful login.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account information was last updated.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents a broad learning subject or programming language module offered on PrepStack.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

name.description: The name of the course (e.g., 'Data Structures', 'Python').

description.description: A brief description of what the course covers.

category.description: The category the course belongs to (e.g., 'Computer Science', 'Programming Language', 'Aptitude').

difficulty.description: The general difficulty level of the course (e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced').

estimatedDurationMinutes.description: Estimated total duration of the course content in minutes.

Module.title: Module
Module.description: Represents a specific learning module within a Course, breaking down the subject into manageable sections.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Module entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this module belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N Module)

name.description: The name of the module (e.g., 'Arrays', 'Basics', 'Intermediate Programming').

description.description: A brief description of the module's content.

order.description: The sequential order of this module within its parent course.

estimatedDurationMinutes.description: Estimated duration of the module content in minutes.

LearningContent.title: LearningContent
LearningContent.description: Represents individual learning items within a Module, such as explanations, code examples, or exercises.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LearningContent entity.

moduleId.description: Reference to the Module this content belongs to. (Relationship: Module 1:N LearningContent)

title.description: The title of the learning content item (e.g., 'Introduction to Arrays', 'Bubble Sort Example').

contentType.description: The type of content (e.g., 'Explanation', 'Code Example', 'Exercise', 'Diagram', 'Quiz', 'Mini Test').

content.description: The actual content, which could be HTML for explanations, code strings, or specific exercise instructions. (Consider using a rich text editor output or markdown).

order.description: The sequential order of this content item within its parent module.

resourceLinks.description: An array of URLs to external resources related to this content.

questionIds.description: References to Question entities if this content includes quizzes or mini tests. (Relationship: Question N:N LearningContent)

LearningProgress.title: LearningProgress
LearningProgress.description: Tracks a user's progress through courses and modules, enabling resume learning and overall progress display.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LearningProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User whose progress is being tracked. (Relationship: User 1:N LearningProgress)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course the user is studying. (Relationship: Course 1:N LearningProgress)

moduleId.description: Reference to the specific Module the user is currently working on or has progressed in. (Relationship: Module 1:N LearningProgress)

learningContentId.description: Reference to the specific LearningContent item the user last interacted with. (Relationship: LearningContent 1:N LearningProgress)

progressPercentage.description: The completion percentage for the current module or course (0-100).

status.description: The current status of the learning (e.g., 'Not Started', 'In Progress', 'Completed').

lastAccessedAt.description: Timestamp when this learning path was last accessed by the user.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when this learning path was completed by the user, if applicable.

Test.title: Test
Test.description: Defines a type of mock test available, including its configuration and associated questions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Test entity.

name.description: The name of the test (e.g., 'Quantitative Aptitude Test', 'Python Skill Test').

description.description: A brief description of what the test assesses.

durationMinutes.description: The allowed duration for the test in minutes.

totalQuestionsAvailable.description: The total number of questions available in the question bank for this test type.

difficulty.description: The general difficulty level of the test (e.g., 'Easy', 'Medium', 'Hard', 'Mixed').

questionPoolIds.description: References to Question entities that form the pool from which test questions are drawn. (Relationship: Question N:N Test)

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a single question with its options, correct answer, and additional metadata, supporting templated generation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

text.description: The main text of the question.

type.description: The type of question (e.g., 'MCQ', 'Numerical', 'Code Output', 'Templated MCQ').

options.description: An array of possible answer options for MCQ questions.

correctAnswer.description: The correct answer or the index of the correct option if it's an MCQ with ordered options.

difficulty.description: The difficulty level of the question (e.g., 'Easy', 'Medium', 'Hard').

topic.description: The specific topic this question belongs to (e.g., 'Arrays', 'Time & Work', 'OOP Concepts').

explanation.description: A detailed explanation for the correct answer.

tags.description: An array of tags for categorizing and filtering questions (e.g., ['DSA', 'Beginner', 'Python']).

templateVariables.description: A JSON string containing key-value pairs for variables that can be randomized in templated questions (e.g., '{ "X": [10, 20, 30], "Y": [2, 4, 5] }').

TestAttempt.title: TestAttempt
TestAttempt.description: Records the details and results of each test taken by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TestAttempt entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who attempted the test. (Relationship: User 1:N TestAttempt)

testId.description: Reference to the type of Test that was attempted. (Relationship: Test 1:N TestAttempt)

attemptedAt.description: Timestamp when the test attempt was completed.

score.description: The score obtained by the user in this test attempt.

totalQuestions.description: The total number of questions presented in this specific test attempt.

correctAnswers.description: The number of questions answered correctly.

wrongAnswers.description: The number of questions answered incorrectly.

unattemptedAnswers.description: The number of questions left unanswered.

accuracyPercentage.description: The accuracy percentage for this test attempt.

attemptPercentage.description: The percentage of questions attempted in the test.

timeTakenSeconds.description: The total time taken by the user to complete the test in seconds.

selectedOptions.description: An array storing the user's selected answers for each question in this attempt (e.g., [{questionId: 'q1', selected: 'A'}, {questionId: 'q2', selected: 'C'}]).

Roadmap.title: Roadmap
Roadmap.description: Defines a structured learning path composed of sequential steps, guiding users through their preparation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Roadmap entity.

name.description: The name of the roadmap (e.g., 'B.Tech Placement Roadmap', 'Programming Starter Roadmap').

description.description: A description outlining the goals and content of the roadmap.

category.description: The category of the roadmap (e.g., 'Placement', 'Skill Development', 'Career Change').

targetAudience.description: Describes the intended audience for this roadmap (e.g., 'B.Tech Students', 'Non-Tech Professionals').

stepIds.description: Ordered references to the RoadmapStep entities that constitute this roadmap. (Relationship: Roadmap 1:N RoadmapStep)

RoadmapStep.title: RoadmapStep
RoadmapStep.description: Represents an individual step within a larger Roadmap, linking to specific learning content or courses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RoadmapStep entity.

roadmapId.description: Reference to the Roadmap this step belongs to. (Relationship: Roadmap 1:N RoadmapStep)

title.description: The title of the roadmap step (e.g., 'Learn Programming Basics', 'Master Data Structures').

description.description: A brief description of what this step entails.

order.description: The sequential order of this step within its parent roadmap.

courseId.description: Optional reference to a Course to be completed as part of this step. (Relationship: Course 1:N RoadmapStep)

moduleId.description: Optional reference to a specific Module to be completed as part of this step. (Relationship: Module 1:N RoadmapStep)

learningContentId.description: Optional reference to a specific LearningContent item for a focused step. (Relationship: LearningContent 1:N RoadmapStep)

Resource.title: Resource
Resource.description: Stores details about downloadable resources like resume templates, cheat sheets, and interview guides.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Resource entity.

title.description: The title of the resource (e.g., 'Modern Tech Resume Template', 'DSA Cheat Sheet').

description.description: A brief description of the resource's content.

type.description: The category type of the resource (e.g., 'Resume Template', 'Cheat Sheet', 'Interview Guide').

category.description: A more specific category for the resource (e.g., 'Fresher', 'DBMS', 'HR Questions').

fileUrl.description: The URL to the actual resource file (e.g., PDF).

fileSizeKB.description: The size of the resource file in kilobytes.

previewImageUrl.description: The URL to an image for previewing the resource.

tags.description: An array of keywords or tags for search and recommendation.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the resource was added.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the resource information was last updated.

ContactSubmission.title: ContactSubmission
ContactSubmission.description: Stores messages submitted through the website's contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContactSubmission entity.

name.description: The name provided by the submitter.

email.description: The email address provided by the submitter for a response.

message.description: The content of the message submitted.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp when the contact form was submitted.

isRead.description: A flag indicating whether the submission has been reviewed by an administrator.

userId.description: Optional reference to the User if the submission was made by a logged-in user. (Relationship: User 1:N ContactSubmission)

definition.description: Stores core user profile information. The document ID `{userId}` corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID. This document contains the user-chosen unique 'userId' (10-char alphanumeric).

A lookup collection to ensure the global uniqueness of the user-chosen 10-character alphanumeric 'userId'. The document ID `{customUserId}` is the user's chosen ID, and the document content maps to the actual Firebase Auth UID. Critical for authorization independence during signup.
A collection containing documents whose IDs correspond to Firebase Auth UIDs of users with administrative privileges. Used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to grant specific admin-only permissions.
Stores definitions for all available learning courses (e.g., 'Python', 'Data Structures'). Read-only for authenticated users.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier for a Course.

definition.description: Stores definitions for modules within a specific course. Read-only for authenticated users. The `courseId` from the parent path is implicitly linked.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Course.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier for a Module.

definition.description: Stores individual learning items (explanations, code snippets, exercises) within a module. Read-only for authenticated users. The `moduleId` from the parent path is implicitly linked.

params.name: courseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent Course.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Module.

params.name: learningContentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a LearningContent item.

definition.description: Tracks a specific user's progress for a particular module, including completion percentage, status, and last accessed content. The document ID `{moduleId}` corresponds to the module being tracked. Includes denormalized 'userId' via path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the student whose progress is being tracked.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the module for which progress is tracked by this document.

definition.description: Stores definitions for all available mock tests (e.g., 'Quantitative Aptitude Test', 'Python Skill Test'). Read-only for authenticated users.

params.name: testId
params.description: The unique identifier for a Test.

definition.description: Stores individual question templates, options, and correct answers for the test engine. Read-only for authenticated users.

params.name: questionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a Question.

definition.description: Records details and results of each test taken by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' via path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the student who made the test attempt.

params.name: testAttemptId
params.description: The unique identifier for a TestAttempt.

definition.description: Stores definitions for structured learning paths, guiding students through preparation. Read-only for authenticated users.

params.name: roadmapId
params.description: The unique identifier for a Roadmap.

definition.description: Represents individual steps within a roadmap, linking to specific learning content or courses. Read-only for authenticated users. The `roadmapId` from the parent path is implicitly linked.

params.name: roadmapId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Roadmap.

params.name: roadmapStepId
params.description: The unique identifier for a RoadmapStep.

definition.description: Stores details about downloadable resources like resume templates, cheat sheets, and interview guides. Read-only for authenticated users.

params.name: resourceId
params.description: The unique identifier for a Resource.

definition.description: Stores messages submitted through the website's contact form. Writeable by any authenticated user, readable only by administrators (as defined in the `/admins` collection).

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a ContactSubmission.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for PrepStack is designed with security, scalability, and debuggability as core priorities, strictly adhering to the provided design principles. The approach leverages path-based authorization, structural segregation, and selective denormalization to achieve these goals.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This is achieved primarily through the use of **path-based ownership** for all user-specific data. Instead of storing a `userId` field within documents and relying on `get()` calls in security rules to verify ownership against a parent document, all private user data is structured as subcollections directly under the user's document:
*   `/users/{userId}/learningProgressByModule/{moduleId}`
*   `/users/{userId}/testAttempts/{testAttemptId}`

In these paths, the `{userId}` wildcard **must** match the authenticated user's Firebase UID (`request.auth.uid`). This means that security rules can simply check `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;` without needing to retrieve any parent document, making operations atomic (suitable for transactions/batches) and rules highly performant and easy to debug. The `userId` field within the `LearningProgress` and `TestAttempt` documents, while present according to the schema, serves as internal data consistency and is not primarily relied upon for *authorization* in rules due to the path structure. This completely eliminates hierarchical authorization dependencies.

To enforce the uniqueness of the `User.userId` (the 10-character alphanumeric ID), a dedicated lookup collection, `/customUserIds/{customUserId}`, is introduced. A user attempting to sign up would first create a document in this collection with their chosen `customUserId` as the document ID. Rules can ensure this `customUserId` does not already exist using `!exists()`, thereby maintaining global uniqueness without complex queries or `get()` calls on potentially large `users` collection documents.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
This design ensures Queryability for Authorized Paths (QAPs) by categorizing data into two primary security postures:

1.  **Global, Read-Only Collections for Authenticated Users:** Collections like `/courses`, `/tests`, `/questions`, `/roadmaps`, and `/resources` (and their subcollections like `/courses/{courseId}/modules`) store public, curriculum-related content. These collections are structured to be entirely readable by *any* authenticated user. This means that a `list` query on `/courses` will return *all* courses, not a filtered subset, satisfying the QAP principle as no server-side filtering is required by security rules. Access is uniformly granted to all logged-in students.

2.  **User-Scoped Collections for Private Data:** Collections like `/users/{userId}/learningProgressByModule` and `/users/{userId}/testAttempts` are inherently user-specific. Any `list` query on these subcollections, e.g., `db.collection('users').doc(uid).collection('learningProgressByModule').get()`, will automatically be scoped to the authenticated user's ID (`uid`). The security rules enforce that `userId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`. This effectively means that `list` operations implicitly return data relevant *only* to the authorized user, perfectly aligning with QAP as no additional filtering logic is needed in rules to prevent data leakage.

**Structural Segregation:**
*   Core `User` profiles are in `/users/{userId}`.
*   Global, static learning content is segregated into top-level collections like `/courses`, `/tests`, `/questions`, `/roadmaps`, and `/resources`. These collections have homogeneous security requirements (read-only for all authenticated users).
*   User-specific progress and attempt data is segregated into subcollections under the user's UID path, ensuring distinct security postures (owner-only access).
*   `ContactSubmission` is a write-only collection for users, readable only by `admins` (a separate `admins` collection provides DBAC for this role).

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Private Data:** Implemented via `/users/{userId}` for user profiles and `/users/{userId}/...` for private subcollections.
*   **Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned Data:** Fully utilized for `learningProgressByModule` and `testAttempts` under `/users/{userId}`.
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** The `/admins/{userId}` collection is used for role-based access control, allowing administrators specific permissions (e.g., reading contact submissions).

This architecture prioritizes clear ownership, eliminates `get()` calls in rules, and enables efficient, secure querying, making the system scalable and highly debuggable.
